summary.intent: Fix a P1 code review finding on PR #664 in fogodev/ars-ui where open drawers were not emitting compensating inert/scroll-lock effects when the `modal` or `prevent_scroll` props changed while the drawer was already open.
summary.outcome: Added regression tests and patched the `SyncProps` transition in the drawer component to correctly emit acquire/release adapter effects based on old context vs new props; formatted, committed as `545f27bb` (formatting fix) plus a follow-up sync commit, and pushed to trigger a new CI run that passed formatting, Clippy, workspace check, mutual exclusion, and snapshots with unit tests still pending.

repo: The drawer component in `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/drawer/mod.rs` uses a `SyncProps` transition branch that was updating `ctx.modal` and `ctx.prevent_scroll` without emitting corresponding adapter effects, unlike the open/close transitions.
CI pipeline order: Formatting → Snapshot Change Check → Workspace Check → Clippy → Mutual Exclusion → Unit Tests; formatting is the gate that must pass first.
Codex bot monitors PRs with 👀 reaction and posts P1 unresolved review threads; the workflow includes replying to Codex threads after each fix push.
Nightly fmt (`cargo +nightly fmt --all`) can require reflowing code that passes clippy, so a separate fmt-check step is necessary before committing.

code.finding: The `SyncProps` branch updates modal/prevent_scroll context but previously emitted no acquire/release intents for inert or scroll-lock adapter effects; fix adds compensating effects based on diff between old context and new props.
Reference region inspected for transition plan structure and context_only usage patterns when understanding how open/close transitions emit adapter effects.
Broader SyncProps and transition implementation context; root cause of missing effects was confirmed here.

workflow: `cargo test -p <crate> <filter>` only accepts a single filter string; multiple test names require a shared prefix or separate invocations.
Write failing regression tests before applying the fix to confirm the bug is captured, then verify tests turn green after the patch.
Polling CI via `gh pr checks <pr> --repo <owner>/<repo>` with sleep intervals is effective but slow; the formatting gate failure can block all subsequent checks, so fix formatting first.
Commit messages for co-authored Codex fixes should include `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>` trailer.

friction: CI blocked entirely on a formatting issue until the nightly fmt fix was pushed, requiring multiple polling cycles before deeper checks could run.
`cargo test` single-filter limitation required re-running tests with a shared prefix instead of listing individual test names.
Long CI polling latency (multiple 90–120s sleep cycles) made the feedback loop slow, especially since Codex review and CI results arrive asynchronously.
Nightly fmt introduced a reflow after the code fix passed tests, requiring an extra fmt + re-check + commit cycle.
open_items: Unit Tests were still pending at the time of the final push; their result was not confirmed in the transcript.
Codex P1 thread response (reply comment) was planned but not shown as completed in the transcript.

Similar `SyncProps` patterns in dialog and popover overlays (`crates/ars-components/src/overlay/dialog/mod.rs`, `crates/ars-components/src/overlay/popover/mod.rs`) were inspected but not audited for the same missing-effects bug.
